nodes.standard_name: Gang de la Brise de Mer
nodes.original_text_name: Gang de la Brise de Mer
nodes.context: Corsican criminal organization founded in the 1970s, based in Northern Corsica, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other illegal activities.
Corsican separatist armed group, suspected to have been targeted by the Brise de Mer in 2001 murders.
Corsican gang based in Ajaccio, dominant in the 2020s, rival of the Brise de Mer coalition.
Corsican criminal group, also known as the Federici clan, involved in the conflict.
Criminal family allied with the Brise de Mer.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate, allied with the Brise de Mer.
Sicilian organized crime syndicate, allied with the Brise de Mer.
Calabrian organized crime syndicate, allied with the Brise de Mer.
Colombian drug cartel, allied with the Brise de Mer.

aliases: National Front for the Liberation of Corsica
nodes.context: Corsican nationalist militant group, allied with the Brise de Mer.
Corsican clan that split from the Brise de Mer after a prison-yard incident in 2000, later supported Richard Casanova's faction.

nodes.original_text_name: group from southern Corsica led by Alain Orsoni
nodes.context: Corsican criminal group from southern Corsica, allied with Richard Casanova's faction.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
edges.context: Brise de Mer is suspected of involvement in the 2001 murders of Armata Corsa members.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
